title: lithocarpus jacobsii
intro: Lithocarpus jacobsii, also known as the Jacobs' tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lithocarpus jacobsii include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Lithocarpus jacobsii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal purposes, and its bark, leaves, and roots are typically used in herbal preparations.
